initiatives.name: Communities United Against Police Brutality (CUAPB)
initiatives.description: A grassroots organization coordinating mutual aid and community support initiatives focused on addressing police brutality in the Twin Cities. They organize direct support for survivors, resource sharing, and community education while building networks of solidarity through advocacy, legal support, and policy reform campaigns.

initiatives.name: Families Supporting Families Against Police Violence
initiatives.description: A grassroots organization fostering mutual aid and community solidarity through support for families affected by police violence in the Twin Cities. They coordinate direct support initiatives, advocacy, and solidarity networks while building power through family support, community organizing, and creating spaces for healing and collective resistance.

initiatives.name: Minnesota Justice Coalition
initiatives.description: A grassroots organization providing legal and advocacy services to advance social justice and racial equity in Minnesota. They coordinate direct support through legal clinics, legislative reform initiatives, and community education while building networks of solidarity through advocacy, policy work, and creating pathways for justice system healing.

initiatives.name:
Cordale Q Handy In Remembrance of Me
              Foundation
initiatives.description: A grassroots organization fostering mutual aid and community solidarity through support for families affected by police violence and advocacy for police accountability in Minnesota. They coordinate direct support initiatives, community healing programs, and solidarity networks while building power through family support, community organizing, and creating spaces for remembrance and systemic change.

initiatives.name: Futures Beyond Criminalization
initiatives.description: A mutual aid collective organizing community-based alternatives to policing and incarceration in Minneapolis. They coordinate direct support through transformative justice practices, community safety initiatives, and resource sharing while building networks of solidarity through political education, community organizing, and advocacy for non-carceral solutions.

initiatives.name: East Side Freedom Library
initiatives.description: A community library fostering mutual aid and solidarity through labor history, immigrant stories, and social justice education in St. Paul. They coordinate educational programs, archival access, and solidarity networks while building power through community knowledge, cultural preservation, and creating spaces for learning and movement building with a focus on labor and immigrant histories.

initiatives.name: May Day Books
initiatives.description: A volunteer-run radical bookstore fostering mutual aid and community power through left-wing literature and political education in Minneapolis's West Bank. They coordinate book access, educational events, and solidarity networks while building power through progressive literature, community gatherings, and creating spaces for political education and movement building since 1975.

initiatives.name: Birch Bark Books
initiatives.description: An Indigenous-owned bookstore fostering mutual aid and community power through Native literature and cultural programming in Minneapolis. They coordinate book access, educational resources, and solidarity networks while building power through Indigenous storytelling, community events, and creating spaces for Native voices and movement building.

initiatives.name: Twin Cities Encampment Responders
initiatives.description: We are individuals coming together to organize eviction defense, cop watch, & mutual aid for the Twin Cities encampments

initiatives.name: Black and Pink MPLS
initiatives.description: Black & Pink MPLS works towards prison abolition in MN by providing pen pal matching to LGBTQIA2S+ folks on the inside.

initiatives.name: Black Garnet Books
initiatives.description: A Black-owned bookstore fostering mutual aid and community power through Black and BIPOC literature in Minneapolis. They coordinate book access, educational resources, and solidarity networks while building power through representation, community events, and creating spaces for BIPOC voices and movement building.

initiatives.name: Boneshaker Books
initiatives.description: A volunteer-run radical bookstore in Minneapolis's Seward neighborhood coordinating mutual aid through accessible radical literature and community space. They organize resource sharing and political education while building networks of solidarity through their bookstore, events, and community programming. Their space serves as a hub for leftist politics, movement support, and collective organizing.

initiatives.name: Blackbird Revolt
initiatives.description: A Black-owned design studio fostering mutual aid and community power through movement-centered design and creative services in Minneapolis. They coordinate design support, resource sharing, and solidarity networks while building power through radical aesthetics, political education, and creating visual tools for social justice movements.

initiatives.name: Rainbow Collective for Change
initiatives.description: An organization building mutual aid networks and community support for LGBTQ+ families with children in the Twin Cities. They coordinate direct support through playgroups, educational programs, and family resources while building inclusive spaces and advocating for LGBTQ+ family equity through community organizing and solidarity networks.

initiatives.name:
TIGERRS (Transgender, Intersex, Gender-Expansive Revolutionary
              Resources & Services)
initiatives.description: TIGERRS is a nonhierarchical collective dedicated to delivering programs and resources that build solidarity and safety among transgender, intersex and gender-expansive Minnesotans.

initiatives.name: Minnesot Transgender Health Coalition
initiatives.description: The Minnesota Transgender Health Coalition is committed to improving health care access and the quality of health care received by trans and gender non-conforming people through education, resources, and advocacy.

initiatives.name: Indigenous Peoples Task Force
initiatives.description: We were founded in 1987 to develop and implement culturally appropriate HIV education and direct services to the Native community in Minnesota.

initiatives.name: Women Against Military Madness (WAMM)
initiatives.description: A grassroots organization coordinating mutual aid and peace initiatives in the Twin Cities. They organize direct support, resource sharing, and community education while building networks of solidarity through anti-war organizing, peace advocacy, and social justice campaigns.

initiatives.name: Southside Harm Reduction Services
initiatives.description: A mutual aid collective providing harm reduction services and community support in Minneapolis. They coordinate direct assistance through syringe access, naloxone distribution, and health resources while building networks of solidarity through peer support, community education, and advocacy for harm reduction approaches to public health.

initiatives.name: Sex Workers Outreach Project (SWOP) Minneapolis
initiatives.description: A mutual aid collective organizing direct support and advocacy for sex workers in Minneapolis. They coordinate resource sharing, community care, and solidarity initiatives while building networks of support through harm reduction, decriminalization advocacy, and community education.

initiatives.name: Racial Justice Network (RJN)
initiatives.description: A multi-racial, grassroots organization fostering mutual aid and community solidarity through racial justice initiatives in the Twin Cities. They coordinate direct support through community relief, crisis response, and educational programs while building networks of solidarity through advocacy, policy reform, and creating spaces for multi-racial coalition building and systemic change.

initiatives.name: Showing Up for Racial Justice (SURJ) - Twin Cities
initiatives.description: A grassroots organization coordinating mutual aid and community support initiatives focused on mobilizing people for racial justice in the Twin Cities. They organize direct action, resource sharing, and political education while building networks of solidarity through community organizing and racial justice advocacy.

initiatives.name: Library Patrons Union
initiatives.description: A grassroots collective organizing mutual aid and community support initiatives to protect and expand public library services in Minneapolis. They coordinate direct action, resource sharing, and community advocacy while building networks of solidarity through patron organizing, library worker support, and defending public spaces as essential community resources.

initiatives.name: St. Paul Mutual Aid
initiatives.description: A grassroots network coordinating mutual aid and community support initiatives in St. Paul. They organize direct assistance, resource sharing, and neighborhood solidarity through their Facebook group, connecting community members to help meet immediate needs while building sustainable networks of care and support.

initiatives.name: Sanctuary Supply Depot
initiatives.description: A mutual aid collective coordinating community resource distribution and support initiatives in Minneapolis. They organize direct assistance through supply distribution, resource sharing, and solidarity networks while building sustainable systems of community care and mutual support.

initiatives.name: AYU
initiatives.description: A mutual aid collective fostering community support and solidarity through direct assistance in Minneapolis. They coordinate resource distribution, community care initiatives, and solidarity networks while building sustainable systems of mutual support and collective empowerment.

initiatives.name: Greater Boston Community Land Trust
initiatives.description: A community land trust fostering mutual aid and housing justice through collective land ownership and community control in Minneapolis. They coordinate affordable housing initiatives, tenant support, and solidarity networks while building power through community ownership, anti-displacement work, and creating spaces for sustainable, community-controlled housing.

initiatives.name: Community Kitchen MPLS
initiatives.description: A mutual aid collective fostering food security through community-based meal preparation and distribution in Minneapolis. They coordinate free meal programs, food resource sharing, and solidarity networks while building power through collective food sovereignty, community care, and creating spaces for nourishment and connection.

initiatives.name: SPIRAL Collective
initiatives.description: Raising money for Lyft rides for abortions and virtual trainings for abortion doulas

initiatives.name: People's Anti-Imperialist Study & Action
initiatives.description: Third-World diasporas in 'mpls/stp' building support for revolutionary struggles/liberation movements in & beyond amerika. Hosts film screenings, teach-ins, and art builds against imperialism.

initiatives.name: Camp Nenookaasi
initiatives.description: A community based healing camp rooted in native practices & inclusive of all unsheltered relatives

initiatives.name: North Star Health Collective
initiatives.description: North Star is a community health collective focused on providing first aid and emergency care at protests, direct actions, and in moments of general upheaval around the Twin Cities.

initiatives.name: Near North Camp Defense
initiatives.description: Help the people living at this encampment in Harrison neighborhood stand against the Minneapolis office of Community Planning & Economic Development— and their brutal enforcers, the infamous Minneapolis Police Department.

initiatives.name: Twin Cities Logistics Collective
initiatives.description: TLC currently works in collaboration with multiple community garden and food distribution projects, as well as home gardeners towards building food resiliency in South Minneapolis.

initiatives.name: Southside Kwes
initiatives.description: Native Women and Two-Spirit led community based Outreach team STOODIS

initiatives.name: Autonomous Yurt Union - Minneapolis
initiatives.description: Autonomous yurt-inspired design builds & deploys in support of unhoused neighbors in the Twin Cities area

initiatives.name: Grapevine Collective
initiatives.description: We’re transforming a church building into a healing justice center, supporting survival programs and revolutionary life in South Minneapolis.

initiatives.name: Autonomous Yurt Union St. Paul
initiatives.description: autonomous yurt-inspired design builds & deploys in support of unhoused neighbors in St Paul

initiatives.name: North Country Food Alliance
initiatives.description: With your help, we share healthy, culturally appropriate food; build community gardens; support local farmers; and reduce commercial and local food waste.

initiatives.name: Sancutary Supply Depot
initiatives.description: We are a central hub aimed at facilitating the collection and distribution of basic survival supplies for our unhoused neighbors.

initiatives.name: Home Base Central
initiatives.description: A collective dedicated to housing justice, support for the unhoused, and mutual aid.
